2604.11733.title: Endogenous Information in Routing Games: Memory-Constrained Equilibria, Recall Braess Paradoxes, and Memory Design
2604.11733.paper_summary: We study routing games in which travelers optimize over routes that are remembered or surfaced, rather than over a fixed exogenous action set. The paper develops a tractable design theory for endogenous recall and then connects it back to an explicit finite-memory micro model. At the micro level, each traveler carries a finite memory state, receives surfaced alternatives, chooses via a logit rule, and updates memory under a policy such as LRU. This yields a stationary Forgetful Wardrop Equilibrium (FWE); existence is proved under mild regularity, and uniqueness follows in a contraction regime for the reduced fixed-point map. The paper's main design layer is a stationary salience model that summarizes persistent memory and interface effects as route-specific weights. Salience-weighted stochastic user equilibrium is the unique minimizer of a strictly convex potential, which yields a clean optimization and implementability theory. In this layer we characterize governed implementability under ratio budgets and affine tying constraints, and derive constructive algorithms on parallel and series-parallel networks. The bridge between layers is exact for last-choice memory (B=1): the micro model is then equivalent to the salience model, so any interior salience vector can be realized by an appropriate surfacing policy. For larger memories, we develop an explicit LRU-to-TTL-to-salience approximation pipeline and add contraction-based bounds that translate surrogate-map error into fixed-point and welfare error. Finally, we define a Recall Braess Paradox, in which improving recall increases equilibrium delay without changing physical capacity, and show that it can arise on every two-terminal network with at least two distinct s-t paths. Targeted experiments support the approximation regime, governed-design predictions, and the computational advantages of the reduced layer.
2604.11733.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究旅行者在記憶或顯示的路線上進行優化的路由遊戲，而不是在固定的外生行動集上。本文發展了一種可處理的內生回憶設計理論，然後將其與明確的有限記憶微觀模型相連接。在微觀層面，每位旅行者攜帶一個有限的記憶狀態，接收顯示的替代選擇，通過邏輯規則進行選擇，並根據如 LRU 的政策更新記憶。這產生了一個穩定的健忘沃德羅普均衡（FWE）；在輕微的正則性下證明了其存在性，並在收縮範疇內得到了唯一性。本文的主要設計層是一個穩定的顯著性模型，將持久記憶和介面效應總結為路線特定的權重。顯著性加權隨機用戶均衡是嚴格凸潛力的唯一最小化者，這產生了一個清晰的優化和可實施性理論。在這一層中，我們在比例預算和仿射綁定約束下刻劃受管制的可實施性，並在並行和串聯-並行網絡上推導出建設性算法。層之間的橋樑對於最後選擇記憶（B=1）是精確的：微觀模型因此等同於顯著性模型，因此任何內部顯著性向量都可以通過適當的顯示政策實現。對於更大的記憶，我們開發了一個明確的 LRU 到 TTL 到顯著性近似管道，並添加了收縮基於的界限，將代理映射誤差轉換為固定點和福利誤差。最後，我們定義了一個回憶布雷斯悖論，其中改善回憶會在不改變物理容量的情況下增加均衡延遲，並顯示它可以在每個具有至少兩條不同 s-t 路徑的雙端網絡上出現。針對性的實驗支持近似範疇、受管制設計預測以及降低層的計算優勢。
2604.11733.author: Saad Alqithami et.al.
